seoTitle: How to Clean Your Email List in Brevo (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to find and remove unengaged Brevo contacts using segments and engagement filters to protect your deliverability and sender reputation.
intro: A bloated contact list full of subscribers who never open your emails quietly drags down your deliverability and wastes sending credits. This guide walks through using Brevo's engagement filters and segmentation tools to identify inactive contacts and decide whether to blocklist or permanently remove them. You'll come away with a repeatable process for keeping your list healthy over time.

steps.heading: Open your contact directory
steps.body: Log into Brevo and go to CRM, then Contacts, to reach your master list of subscribers.

steps.heading: Filter for unengaged contacts
steps.body: Use the Add filter tool to select Contact attributes, then Engagement status, and choose Unengaged. Layer in a Creation date filter to isolate older signups who never interacted.

steps.heading: Review the matching contacts
steps.body: Run the filter and let Brevo return the count and list of contacts matching your unengaged criteria before taking any action.

steps.heading: Choose blocklist or delete
steps.body: Select all matching contacts and either blocklist them, which is reversible and keeps historical data, or permanently delete them if you need strict data removal.

steps.heading: Set up a recurring cleanup routine
steps.body: Repeat this filtering process on a regular schedule, or build a simple automation, so your list stays clean without manual effort every time.

faq.question: Does blocklisting delete my contact data?
faq.answer: No, blocklisting keeps the contact record but stops future emails from being sent to them, and it can be reversed later.

faq.question: Can I undo a permanent delete in Brevo?
faq.answer: No, permanent deletion removes the contact record entirely and cannot be undone, so use it only when you specifically need to purge data.

faq.question: How often should I clean my Brevo list?
faq.answer: Running a cleanup on a regular rolling basis, such as monthly or quarterly, helps keep your sender score healthy and avoids paying to email inactive contacts.

faq.question: Will cleaning my list hurt my subscriber count?
faq.answer: Your total count may drop, but your open and click rates typically improve since you're no longer emailing unresponsive addresses.
